summary.title: Brew & Bloom Coffee Co.
summary.description: Threat Dragon project for Brew & Bloom Coffee Co. based on Section 5.2 data flows, Section 5.1 trust boundaries, and Section 6/Table 2 threats of the provided threat model document

data.name: Customer
data.description: Customer using the public ordering interface.

data.name: Payment Gateway
data.description: External and partially trusted third-party payment service.

data.name: Frontend
data.description: Client-side web interface. Client side is untrusted; all input must be validated on the server.

threats.title: T-001 - Customer Account Spoofing
threats.description: An attacker could impersonate a legitimate customer to place fraudulent orders or access their order history.

threats.title: T-002 - Staff Account Spoofing
threats.description: An attacker could impersonate staff to manipulate order statuses, access sales reports, or modify menu items and application configurations.

threats.title: T-003 - Web Application Server Spoofing
threats.description: An attacker might set up a fraudulent web server to phish user credentials or intercept sensitive data.

data.name: Web Application Server
data.description: Backend Node.js REST API. Authenticates users, enforces authorization, processes business logic, and communicates with the database and payment service.

threats.title: T-001 - Customer Account Spoofing
threats.description: An attacker could impersonate a legitimate customer to place fraudulent orders or access their order history.

threats.title: T-002 - Staff Account Spoofing
threats.description: An attacker could impersonate staff to manipulate order statuses, access sales reports, or modify menu items and application configurations.

threats.title: T-003 - Web Application Server Spoofing
threats.description: An attacker might set up a fraudulent web server to phish user credentials or intercept sensitive data.

threats.title: T-004 - Order Data Tampering
threats.description: An attacker could modify order details (e.g., items, quantities, prices, status) in transit or at rest.

threats.title: T-005 - Menu Data Tampering
threats.description: An attacker could alter menu item data (e.g., prices or descriptions) before it is stored in the database.

threats.title: T-006 - Sales Report Tampering
threats.description: Unauthorized individuals could alter sales data in the MongoDB Database, leading to falsified reports.

threats.title: T-007 - Application Code/Configuration Tampering
threats.description: Malicious modification of the application’s source code, server configurations, or environment variables could introduce backdoors or disable security features.

threats.title: T-008 - Fraudulent Order Repudiation
threats.description: A customer could deny placing an order if insufficient logging and non-repudiation controls are in place.

threats.title: T-009 - Staff Action Repudiation
threats.description: Staff members (Barista, Manager, Administrator) could deny performing actions if audit logs are insufficient, not immutable, or can be tampered with.

threats.title: T-010 - Customer PII Disclosure
threats.description: Unauthorized access to customer names, contact details, delivery addresses, and order history.

threats.title: T-011 - Staff Credential Disclosure
threats.description: Exposure of usernames, passwords, or session tokens for staff members due to insecure storage or unencrypted transmission.

threats.title: T-012 - Application Code and Configuration Disclosure
threats.description: Exposure of source code, server configurations, environment variables, or API keys.

threats.title: T-013 - Sales Data Disclosure
threats.description: Unauthorized access to sales reports or underlying aggregated sales data.

threats.title: T-014 - Web Application Server DoS
threats.description: An attacker could flood the Web Application Server with excessive requests, making it unavailable.

threats.title: T-015 - MongoDB Database DoS
threats.description: Malicious or poorly optimized queries could overload the database, making it unresponsive.

threats.title: T-016 - Customer to Staff Privilege Escalation
threats.description: A customer could exploit a vulnerability to gain unauthorized access to Barista, Manager, or Administrator functionalities.

threats.title: T-017 - Barista/Manager to Administrator Privilege Escalation
threats.description: A Barista or Manager could exploit a vulnerability to gain Administrator privileges.

threats.title: T-018 - Horizontal Privilege Escalation
threats.description: Gaining unauthorized access to another user’s account within the same privilege level.

data.name: MongoDB Database
data.description: Persistent data store trusted only for calls from the web application server identity.

threats.title: T-001 - Customer Account Spoofing
threats.description: An attacker could impersonate a legitimate customer to place fraudulent orders or access their order history.

threats.title: T-002 - Staff Account Spoofing
threats.description: An attacker could impersonate staff to manipulate order statuses, access sales reports, or modify menu items and application configurations.

threats.description: An attacker could alter menu item data (e.g., prices or descriptions) before it is stored in the database.

threats.title: T-006 - Sales Report Tampering
threats.description: Unauthorized individuals could alter sales data in the MongoDB Database, leading to falsified reports.

threats.title: T-008 - Fraudulent Order Repudiation
threats.description: A customer could deny placing an order if insufficient logging and non-repudiation controls are in place.

threats.title: T-009 - Staff Action Repudiation
threats.description: Staff members (Barista, Manager, Administrator) could deny performing actions if audit logs are insufficient, not immutable, or can be tampered with.

threats.title: T-010 - Customer PII Disclosure
threats.description: Unauthorized access to customer names, contact details, delivery addresses, and order history.

threats.title: T-011 - Staff Credential Disclosure
threats.description: Exposure of usernames, passwords, or session tokens for staff members due to insecure storage or unencrypted transmission.

threats.title: T-013 - Sales Data Disclosure

threats.title: T-015 - MongoDB Database DoS
threats.description: Malicious or poorly optimized queries could overload the database, making it unresponsive.

data.name: Order details
data.description: Frontend sends order details to the web application server.

data.name: Tokenized payment info
data.description: Web application server initiates payment request to the third-party payment gateway.

data.name: Transaction response
data.description: Payment gateway returns payment success/failure to the web application server.

data.name: Create order / payment status
data.description: If payment succeeds, web application server records order details and payment status in MongoDB.

data.name: Stored order record
data.description: MongoDB stores the new order record and returns persistence result.

data.name: Order confirmation / status
data.description: Web application server sends order confirmation and status update to the customer frontend.

data.name: Select items / submit order
data.description: Customer selects items, customizes them, and submits order details to the frontend.

data.name: Order confirmation / status
data.description: Web application server sends order confirmation and status update to the customer frontend.

cells.description: Barista logs in through the staff interface and requests incoming orders.
Frontend sends login and order-view request to the backend.
Backend authenticates and authorizes the barista, then queries MongoDB for relevant order data.
MongoDB returns pending order data to the backend.
Backend sends the order list to the barista interface.
Barista views incoming orders.
Barista selects an order and changes its status.
Frontend sends updated order status to the backend.
Backend validates the new status and updates the order record in MongoDB.
Manager using the staff interface.
Client-side web interface. Client side is untrusted; all input must be validated on the server.

cells.description: Manager logs in through the staff interface and requests basic sales reports.
Frontend sends login and report request to the backend.
Backend authenticates and authorizes the manager, then queries MongoDB for aggregated sales data.
MongoDB returns requested sales data.
Backend formats totals and sends the basic sales report to the manager interface.
Manager views the basic sales report in the browser.
Administrator using the staff interface.
Client-side web interface. Client side is untrusted; all input must be validated on the server.

cells.description: Administrator logs in and navigates to the menu management section.
Administrator enters new menu item details and submits the form.
Frontend sends new menu item details to the backend.
Backend validates the data, authorizes the request, and sends the new menu item to MongoDB for creation.
MongoDB creates the new menu item record and returns the result.
Backend returns successful menu item creation confirmation.
Administrator receives confirmation that the menu item has been added.
